title: How to Optimize Performance for Verilator 5.040 (Significant Degradation vs. 5.026)?
body:
Project Background
Current Version: Long-term use of Verilator 5.026 with stable simulation performance ~78 CPS,(Cycles Per Second).
Design Scale:
~30 million signals, with dozens of HierBlock (hierarchical) modules.
After applying --hier-block-dpi cost-estimate, performance in 5.026 reaches 100 CPS.
Hardware Configuration:
256-core CPU, with sufficient resources and high parallelization potential.
Performance Issue
After upgrading to Verilator 5.040:

Performance drops by 30%-40% (down to 52 CPS).
Tested versions (5.028, 5.034, 5.040) all fail to match 5.026’s performance.
Current Compilation Flags
```BASH
verilator --vpi -Wno-lint \
    --build-jobs 31 --verilate-jobs 32 \
    --threads 48 --threads-dpi all \
    --clk GLB_REF_CLK --no-timing -cc \
    --threads-max-tasks 45000 \
    --hierarchical --bbox-unsup
```
Attempted Optimizations
Threading Parameter Adjustments

Tuned --threads and --threads-max-tasks:
No performance gain when threads >48 or tasks >45,000 (likely hitting CPU scheduling limits).
Profile-Guided Optimization (PGO)

Used --prof-pgo and hier_workers for per-module threading:
Only minor improvement (~70 CPS), still worse than 5.026.
Anomalies Observed:
Enabling hier_workers for most modules degrades performance.
Only a single module benefits from multi-threading, but the improvement is marginal.
DPI Cost Calculation Issue

Problem Identified:
In V3ProtectLib.cpp, the profile_data assigns the same cost value to both combo_update and seq_update, which is clearly incorrect.
Temporary Fix:
Manually divided DPI cost by 2, leading to performance gains—but this is a hacky solution.
Question:
Is there a more accurate DPI cost calculation method? Can this be fixed via parameter tuning or a code patch?
Why is the performance of version 5.040 so much worse than that of 5.026?
Do you have any other performance optimization suggestions?
